ksa_text: Skill in designing and delivering data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, including role-appropriate content, instructional methods, and curriculum development.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Operations & Program Management is a primary or secondary domain for the AI Innovation Lead. This skill KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (senior specialist).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Operations & Program Management), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.504). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the AI Innovation Lead versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: PMI, TOGAF operational frameworks, and CDO survey data (NewVantage Partners) confirm operational program management as a key competency for data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Operations & Program Management appearing in job postings and role definition literature for AI Innovation Lead.

decision_rationale: Operations & Program Management skill KSA is within scope for the AI Innovation Lead at the senior specialist tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
